australia.title: US Supreme Court curbs race-based voting maps in landmark ruling
summary: Summarize this news article:

The US Supreme Court has sharply limited the use of race in drawing electoral districts, in a landmark decision that could reshape congressional maps nationwide and boost Republican prospects ahead of midterm elections.

In a 6–3 ruling split along ideological lines, the court struck down a map creating a second majority-Black district in Louisiana, finding it amounted to an unconstitutional racial gerrymander despite being drawn to comply with the 1965 Voting Rights Act.

The decision leaves the core of the Voting Rights Act intact but narrows how it can be used, a decision that will be seen by civil rights activists as a blow to a landmark civil rights law that has already been weakened by previous rulings over the past decade.
It was not immediately clear how much the ruling will impact the November elections for control of Congress.
The ruling could help Republicans under Donald Trump redraw electoral maps to tilt a number of seats in their favour.(AP Photo: Julia Demaree Nikhinson)
Summarize this news article:

Writing for the conservative majority, Justice Samuel Alito said that compliance with the law "could not justify" the use of race in this case, arguing that Section 2 of the act does not require states to draw districts primarily on racial lines.

"In sum, because the Voting Rights Act did not require Louisiana to create an additional majority-minority district, no compelling interest justified the state's use of race in creating SB8," Alito wrote.
"That map is an unconstitutional gerrymander, and its use would violate the plaintiffs' constitutional rights."

The ruling marks a significant shift in how courts interpret the balance between preventing racial discrimination and adhering to constitutional guarantees of equal protection, raising the bar for when race can be considered in re-districting.
The Supreme Court's hearing came on the same day politicians in Florida were meeting to redraw that state's electoral maps, potentially to favour the ruling Republican Party.(AP: Mike Stewart)

australia.title: Hanson billed taxpayers for flights to fundraiser, to catch Florida jet
summary: Summarize this news article:

One Nation leader Pauline Hanson billed taxpayers $2,000 for flights from Hobart to Brisbane after a fundraising event just in time to catch a private jet to Florida paid for by Gina Rinehart, for a US jaunt courtesy of the mining magnate.
In October last year Senator Hanson took a $768 flight from Brisbane to Hobart, where she stayed for four nights and opened the Bass branch of One Nation with her daughter, Lee.
Senator Hanson also hosted an afternoon tea with her daughter to "help raise funds to our new Bass Branch" and for Teen Challenge Tasmania.
Lee Hanson and Pauline Hanson held a fundraiser for the party while the senator was in Hobart.(Facebook: Lee Hanson for TAS Senate — Pauline Hanson's One Nation)
On October 26, Senator Hanson flew from Hobart to Melbourne and then to Brisbane, at a cost to taxpayers of $1,990, before a private trip to Florida that same day paid by Mrs Rinehart.
Senator Hanson has defended Rinehart-sponsored flights as saving the taxpayer money.
"These fli Summarize this news article:
ghts are no cost to the taxpayer," Senator Hanson said in March.
"It didn't cost the taxpayer a cent for me to fly on a plane — are you worried about whose plane it was on?"
But the taxpayer was billed for the legs of her travel up to Brisbane to catch the jet paid by Mrs Rinehart.
Senator Hanson also billed taxpayers $505 for a Comcar upon her return from Florida to Brisbane.
Senator Hanson has been contacted for comment.

Taxpayers also footed the bill for a four-day round trip in mid-October that included a visit to Tamworth for a fundraising event and to woo disgruntled Nationals MP Barnaby Joyce, as well as to Melbourne to attend the opening of a Gina Rinehart-funded student accommodation block.

Senator Hanson claimed a $650 flight from Brisbane to Sydney on Friday, October 17, staying overnight, then a $372 flight from Sydney to Tamworth on Saturday, the same day that Mr Joyce declared he would not recontest his seat of New England for the Nationals and was mulling a defection to

australia.title: China to help Australia with jet fuel supplies, Wong says
summary: Summarize this news article:
Foreign Minister Penny Wong says the Chinese government has agreed to work with Australian businesses on jet fuel supplies but provided little detail about what that would look like.
"I can confirm the Chinese government is facilitating engagement with Australian businesses on jet fuel," she said during a press conference in Beijing.
“This is an important step, however, it is the first step.
“The whole reason I’m here is to try and press for and advocate for Australia’s interests and for the provision of liquid fuels."
The war in Iran and the closure of the Strait of Hormuz has left Australia and many other countries across the Asia-Pacific region facing an energy crisis.
Senator Wong met with her Chinese counterpart Wang Yi in Beijing on Wednesday where she said they needed to collaborate on fuel supply issues.
China was a major exporter of jet fuel and diesel to Australia but paused exports at the start of the Middle East conflict to protect its own domestic supplies.
Senator Wong wo Summarize this news article:
uld not elaborate on which specific Australian companies China had agreed to work with.
She said she would keep "advocating for Australian businesses and the Australian economy".
"Australia will continue to cooperate where we can, disagree where we must and engage in the national interest," Senator Wong said earlier in the day.
"It is more important than ever to work together to keep fuel and goods flowing, and to uphold the peace, prosperity and security of our region.

"The inputs China provides to Australia, including aviation fuel, support, Australia's resources sector, which in turn helps to maintain the flow of commodities which underpin our bilateral trading relationship."

The foreign minister met with her Japanese counterpart Toshimitsu Motegi, the Minister of Trade, Economy and Industry Ryosei Akazawa and the Minister of Economic Security Kimi Onoda and other senior ministers in Tokyo on Tuesday.
Senator Wong will visit South Korea on Thursday on her regional tour where she is

india.title: TN exit polls: AK breakthrough or PK's 'farsh' moment — what will it be for Vijay?
summary: Summarize this news article:
NEW DELHI: Exit poll projections for Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK) have thrown up one of the most intriguing questions of the 2026 Tamil Nadu elections: is
on the cusp of a breakthrough like Arvind Kejriwal in Delhi, or will he resemble Prashant Kishor's Bihar experiment that ended in 'arsh se farsh par'.
The answer, at least today, depends on which exit poll one chooses to believe.
Most projections place TVK in the 10â24 seat range, a significant debut. According to most pollsters Vijay's party will be one that positions it as a spoiler rather than a main contender.
P-Marq and Matrize projected 10â12 seats for TVK, while Peoples Pulse gives it a stronger 18â24, suggesting meaningful traction among urban and youth voters.
In this scenario, Vijayâs party could end up splitting anti-incumbent votes, indirectly aiding the DMK.
Axis MyIndia projected a dramatic surge, giving TVK 98â120 seats - numbers that, if turned into reality on May 4, would catapult Vijay into the centre of Summarize this news article:
Tamil Nadu politics overnight.
That would place him in Kejriwal territory: a first-time entrant not just breaking through, but becoming the centre of state politics.
And Vijay also has his superstar Thalaivar image - something that has worked in Tamil Nadu for years with the rise of MG Ramachandran and the J Jayalalithaa.

However, unlike Delhi in 2013, where Kejriwal rode a singular anti-corruption wave, Tamil Nadu's contest is layered, entrenched in Dravidian politics, strong party machinery, and decaded-old voter loyalties. Even a strong showing for TVK does not automatically translate into power unless it converts popularity into booth-level efficiency, an area where established players like the DMK and AIADMK retain a clear edge.
However, if these exit polls turn out to be wrong on May 4, and Vijay's grand political debut becomes a flop show, it would be similar to what we saw last year in Bihar.
While there was much discussion around political mastermind Prashant Kishor's debut,

india.title: Bengal exit polls: BJP, TMC in close fight, hung assembly likely; Mamata staring at setback
summary: Summarize this news article:
NEW DELHI: Exit polls for the 2026 West Bengal assembly elections on Wednesday pointed to a sharply divided verdict, with most projections giving the Bharatiya Janata Party (
) an edge, even as one survey indicated a strong win for the ruling Trinamool Congress (

Poll of polls suggested a neck-and-neck fight between the two main contenders, projecting 145 seats each for the BJP and the TMC in the 294-member assembly, with others expected to win around four seats.
Tensions Explode in South 24 Parganas as Mamata Banerjee Alleges CRPF Excess During Polling

However, a majority of individual surveys tilted towards the BJP. Chanakya Strategies projected the BJP at 150â160 seats and the TMC at 130â140. Matrize gave the BJP 146â161 seats against the TMCâs 125â140, while P-MARQ projected a wider margin with the BJP at 150â175 and the TMC at 118â138.
JVC indicated a closer race but still put the BJP ahead with 138â159 seats, compared to the TMCâs 131â152. Smaller parties and  Summarize this news article:
others were projected to win only a handful of seats across most surveys.
In contrast, Peopleâs Pulse stood out as an outlier, projecting a decisive lead for the TMC with 178â187 seats, while placing the BJP at 95â110 seats and others at 2â3.
The Left Front, Congress and others, are projected to win only a handful of seats across surveys.

Taken together, the projections suggest a bipolar contest, with the BJP appearing to have gained significant ground against the incumbent TMC, which is seeking a fourth consecutive term under chief minister

The exit poll projections come after voting for the final phase concluded on Wednesday evening, with voter turnout touching around 90%, reflecting strong participation across districts. Purba Bardhaman led with over 92% turnout, followed by Hooghly, Nadia and Howrah.
The high-voltage campaign saw both sides claiming momentum, with the TMC asserting that the turnout reflected support for its governance, while the BJP argued it indicated a wa

india.title: Justice dept sues $5B tech firm for excluding Americans from applying to high-paying jobs
summary: Summarize this news article:

The US Department of Justice (DOJ) has sued California-based technology company Cloudera for allegedly discriminating against Americans from applying to high-paying technology jobs. The department claims that the company created a separate recruitment and hiring process to âdeter US workers from applyingâ, adding that it âalso did not consider them for lucrative technology jobs that the company earmarked for people with temporary employment visaâ. It further claimed that Cloudera created an email account that did not allow external emails, but still instructed applicants to use that unworkable email address to apply for jobs.

For those unaware, Cloudera is a data software company that was acquired by private equity firms KKR and Clayton, Dubilier & Rice in October 2021 for approximately $5.3 billion. It is no longer a publicly traded company.
Hereâs the official announcement made by DOJ suing Cloudera

india.title: Kerala exit polls: UDF predicted to win coastal state in neck-and-neck contest with LDF
summary: Summarize this news article:
NEW DELHI: With not a very large gap between Left-led LDF and the Congress-led UDF, the exit polls have predicted a neck and neck contest between both the rival factions.
According to the poll of polls, UDF is expected to cross the majority mark with 72 seats in the 140 Kerala assembly.
However, the LDF is expected to be not far behind the Congress-led faction with pollsters suggesting 63 seats for the ruling Front.
Axis My India has predicted the widest margin between the LDF and the UDF, with 55 and 83 seats, respectively.
PMARQ exit poll, on the other hand, has predicted majority for the ruling LDF with 75 seats.
In Kerala, the Congress-led UDF is seeking to replace the LDF government that is in power.

Keralaâs electoral history has typically been defined by alternating LDF and UDF govts. However, LDFâs consecutive victory in 2021 under Vijayan disrupted that pattern, making the 2026 contest a test of whether traditional anti-incumbency sentiment remains intact.
A defeat for LDF Summarize this news article:
would also diminish the presence of CPM in its last major stronghold.

The campaign narrative has also undergone notable shifts. Early phases were dominated by issues such as corruption allegations, economic concerns, backdoor appointments, the Sabarimala gold heist controversy, and rehabilitation concerns of Wayanad landslide victims, alongside scandals involving opposition leaders.

However, as campaigning intensified, these issues gave way to sharper political attacks, including allegations of covert alliances, communal polarisation debates, and direct exchanges between senior leaders.

LDF has framed the election as a referendum on the âPinarayi modelâ, highlighting welfare expansion, infrastructure development, and administrative centralisation. It has also promised an expanded welfare agenda if voted back to governance.
UDF, on the other hand, has advanced a counter-narrative of âwelfare with accountabilityâ, questioning the efficiency, transparency, and fiscal sustainabili
